name: From Betera to Lliria — Circular Walk
name_original: De Bétera a Llíria — Paseo circular
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Bétera are set amidst a varied landscape, characterized by the foothills of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park and surrounding agricultural and natural terrains. The region features a mix of Mediterranean habitats, including ravines, pine forests, and cork oak trees, alongside cultivated fields. This environment provides diverse terrain for touring cyclists, ranging from paved roads to trails. Best touring cycling routes around Bétera The most popular touring cycling route is From Betera to Lliria — Circular Walk, a 24.9 miles (40.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a journey through varied local landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Recreation area – Recreation area loop from Horta Vella, a difficult 27.1 miles (43.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes, characteristic of the Sierra Calderona foothills. Local touring cyclists also love the Marines Viejo – Ojos Negros Greenway loop from Bétera, a 68.9 miles (110.9 km) trail leading through diverse natural and agricultural scenery, often completed in about 7 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Bétera is defined by the challenging climbs of the Sierra Calderona, scenic agricultural lands, and routes along ravines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Bétera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from 51 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bétera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Bétera?
A: Bétera offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with approximately 39 routes available for exploration. These routes cater to various skill levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Bétera?
A: The terrain around Bétera is quite varied, ranging from challenging climbs in the foothills of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Calderona Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; to scenic rides through agricultural lands and along ravines. You'll encounter a mix of paved roads and trails, set amidst Mediterranean habitats with pine forests and cork oak trees.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Bétera?
A: Yes, Bétera has several routes suitable for beginners. There are 8 easy touring cycling routes that offer a gentler introduction to the region's landscapes. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes around Bétera?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Bétera offers 17 difficult routes, many of which feature significant elevation changes characteristic of the Sierra Calderona. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31789090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Recreation area – Recreation area loop from Horta Vella&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 43.6 km with substantial climbing. The Oronet Pass within the Sierra Calderona is also a notable ascent offering panoramic views.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in Bétera?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Bétera are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918524812/from-betera-to-lliria-circular-walk" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Betera to Lliria — Circular Walk&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 40.1 km route. Another is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31789090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Recreation area – Recreation area loop from Horta Vella&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Bétera?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Bétera is typically during &lt;strong&gt;April and May&lt;/strong&gt;. During these months, the weather is generally pleasant, making it ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes of the region and the Sierra Calderona Natural Park.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Bétera's routes offer access to a rich array of landmarks. You can explore ancient 'catxerulos' (old stone structures), traditional farmhouses, and even Roman thermal bath remains at Horta Vella. The imposing &lt;strong&gt;Castillo de Bétera&lt;/strong&gt;, built on an Arab watchtower, is a significant historical site. For natural beauty, routes often pass through the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, offering views from spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that take me towards Valencia city?
A: Yes, Bétera's strategic location allows for routes that connect towards Valencia. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32107269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serranos Towers – Queen's Square loop from Horta Vella&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 30.8 km route that can lead you towards the city, allowing you to combine outdoor adventures with urban exploration. You can also access the vast Turia Gardens in Valencia, which are ideal for cycling.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Bétera?
A: The touring cycling routes in Bétera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from 51 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse natural and agricultural scenery, the challenging climbs of the Sierra Calderona, and the variety of routes available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there options for longer touring cycling trips from Bétera?
A: Absolutely. For those looking for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6914870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marines Viejo – Ojos Negros Greenway loop from Bétera&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 110.9 km trail that takes approximately 7 hours 30 minutes to complete, leading through diverse natural and agricultural scenery.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with public transport in Bétera?
A: Yes, Bétera is well-connected, and you can combine your cycling trips with public transport. Bicycles are permitted on the Metro during specific hours, which facilitates exploring wider areas, including Valencia city, and can help you access different starting points for your touring routes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by cafes or places to rest?
A: Many routes in the Bétera area pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes and places to rest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10399250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Café in Spain – Municipal park loop from Horta Vella&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 61.6 km route that suggests opportunities for breaks. The region's agricultural landscape often means you're not far from local amenities.
tips: Nice park just a few km from the urban center of Llíria. It has tables and benches and can be reached by a bike path that runs alongside the road towards Olocau. A beautiful place to rest or spend a day with nature.
Its construction of Islamic origin, used to be a strategic fortress for defense. Today you can visit, even inside there are some municipal offices.
